title: lycopodioides douglasii
intro: Lycopodioides douglasii, also known as Douglas' club moss, is a species of club moss that belongs to the genus Lycopodium. It is a small, evergreen plant that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Lycopodioides douglasii is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis, and may also have anti-cancer and antioxidant properties.
